name: Helm of Hades Extended
description: The Helm of Hades, sometimes referred to as the Cap of Invisibility, is a powerful artifact of immense significance in Greek mythology. Forged by the Cyclopes during the Titanomachy as a token of gratitude to Hades, the god of the Underworld, the helm grants its wearer absolute invisibility, rendering them undetectable to both mortals and gods. This extended version incorporates additional enchantments, allowing the wearer to not only become invisible but also to manipulate shadows, creating illusions and even momentarily stepping between the realm of the living and the dead. Its power is so formidable that even Zeus himself would struggle to perceive someone concealed by it. The Helm played a pivotal role in the war against the Titans, aiding the Olympian gods in their victory. It was used by Perseus during his quest to slay Medusa, allowing him to approach the Gorgon undetected and sever her head. The helm's association with Hades reinforces his authority over the unseen and his dominion over the Underworld, representing the ultimate power of concealment and the ability to operate beyond the perception of others. Possessing the Helm of Hades Extended grants unparalleled stealth and strategic advantage, making it a coveted artifact throughout mythological history, though its potent power comes with the inherent risk of misuse and corruption.
subtitle: Invisibility and Shadow Manipulation
origin: Forged by the Cyclopes for Hades during the Titanomachy.
currentLocation: Unknown, presumed to be hidden within the Underworld.

keyMyths.title: The Titanomachy
keyMyths.description: The Helm was created by the Cyclopes as a gift to Hades, granting him invisibility during the war against the Titans, significantly aiding the Olympians.

keyMyths.title: Perseus and Medusa
keyMyths.description: Perseus used the Helm of Hades to approach Medusa undetected, allowing him to sever her head without being turned to stone by her gaze.

sources.author: Apollodorus
sources.description: A comprehensive source on Greek mythology.
sources.text: A comprehensive source on Greek mythology.
